name: Whole Wheat Pizza Crust
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir the dry yeast into the warm water and set aside for 5 to 10 minutes, until it becomes foamy.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, whole wheat flour, salt, and cornmeal (if using).
3. Add the yeast mixture to the flour mixture. Mix with your hands or a wooden spoon until the dough begins to pull away from the sides of the bowl.
4. If the dough is too sticky, add a little more flour, a tablespoon at a time, until it is no longer tacky.
5. Divide the dough into two equal portions. Flatten each portion into a ball on a lightly floured surface.
6. Roll out each ball of dough to a 15-inch circle. The dough should overlap the edges of the pan by about 1 inch all around.
7. If the dough is difficult to roll, let it rest for a few minutes and try again. You can refrigerate or freeze the rolled-out dough before baking. Thaw in the refrigerator before rolling out.
8. Coat two nonstick pizza pans with cooking spray and sprinkle with cornmeal (if desired).
9. Place each rolled-out crust on a prepared pan and add your favorite toppings.
[ MODERN MATURITY: Feb/March 1990 ]
Posted By: Fred Peters
